macro_events.event_summary: U.S. imposed new sanctions on companies and individuals in the Middle East and China accused of helping Iran, tightening enforcement of Iran-related restrictions.
primary_drivers: Tighter enforcement reduces Iranian oil/petrochemical flows reaching global markets, supporting crude prices
Geopolitical risk premium on Middle East assets
Secondary sanctions risk on Chinese intermediaries raises US-China trade tension

beneficiaries.rationale: Sanctions on Iran-enabling entities tighten effective Iranian crude/petrochemical exports; marginal supply removal supports Brent. ExxonMobil's upstream earnings have direct leverage to crude price (~$1B EBIT per ~$10/bbl).
Pure-play US shale producer with high crude price sensitivity; benefits from any tightening of global supply via Iran enforcement.
Escalating Iran sanctions enforcement and geopolitical posture supports defense spending narrative; Lockheed has direct Middle East FMS exposure (PAC-3, THAAD, F-35).
Oilfield services leveraged to higher oil prices driving capex; Halliburton has high North America completion exposure that responds to crude moves.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of opex for legacy carriers; any crude price uplift from tighter Iran enforcement compresses near-term margins. Delta lacks the hedging protection of some peers.

headlines: U.S. sanctions companies and individuals in the Middle East and China for helping Iran
